name: Pooled ED/BPH studies did not show increased hypotension or MACE with antihypertensive co-use
statement: A pooled analysis of 72 phase II-IV ED/BPH studies found no evidence that tadalafil alongside non-alpha-blocker antihypertensive medications increased hypotension-related adverse events or major adverse cardiovascular events compared with antihypertensive medications alone in the studied populations.
population_or_context: ED and/or BPH trial participants, stratified by antihypertensive medication use.
time_horizon: Phase II-IV clinical study exposure periods pooled by the authors.
quantitative_note: In all placebo-controlled studies, hypotension-related TEAEs ranged from 0.6% to 1.5% and MACE from 0.0% to 1.0% across indications. Tadalafil increased hypotension-related TEAEs only in the ED as-needed group without antihypertensive medication (p = .0070), while no significant difference was reported among patients receiving one or more antihypertensive medications (p >= .7386). MACE incidence did not differ significantly between tadalafil and placebo groups with or without antihypertensive medications (p >= .2238).

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports pooled sample sizes, hypotension-related TEAE and MACE incidence ranges, p values, and conclusion.

caveats: The analysis excludes alpha-blockers from the antihypertensive co-use question.
No detected added risk in selected ED/BPH trials is not proof of safety in high-risk cardiovascular patients or off-label enhancement users.
Industry conflicts are disclosed in the source.
population_profile: Patients with ED and/or BPH, including antihypertensive medication users; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 except where they share antihypertensive exposure.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil regimens across phase II-IV ED/BPH studies.
indication_context: On-label ED/BPH pooled safety analysis used as off-label interaction context.
comparator_context: Placebo-controlled tadalafil versus placebo comparisons, stratified by antihypertensive medication use.
endpoint_measure: Hypotension-related treatment-emergent adverse events and major adverse cardiovascular events.
effect_estimate: No significant tadalafil-placebo difference in hypotension-related TEAEs among participants receiving at least one antihypertensive medication (p >= .7386) and no significant MACE difference with or without antihypertensive medication use (p >= .2238).
safety_findings: Common non-alpha-blocker antihypertensive co-use did not show an added hypotension or MACE signal in pooled trial populations, but this does not override label cautions or alpha-blocker/nitrate contraindication boundaries.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for men age 30 to 50 taking common antihypertensives, low to moderate for healthy off-label users without ED/BPH.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Pooled heterogeneous trials, trial selection/exclusion criteria, industry involvement, abstract-only extraction, and incomplete directness for off-label populations.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil safety or interaction evidence; transfer from other PDE5 inhibitors is not needed, but monitored trial or clinical-population context limits off-label healthy-user risk estimates.
